llm_result.summary: Clinical picture centers on acute aversive reactivity to chewing noises, surge of anger, intrusive replay of trigger sounds, self-directed distress, and a parallel texture sensitivity. The following modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers span sensory integrity, autonomic regulation, cognitive-emotional processing, sleep, environment, social supports, and digital self-monitoring. Each lever targets specific criteria (C01-C05), proposes measurable outcomes, and notes feasibility and safety considerations to guide staged implementation without pharmacology or unsafe practices.

solutions.solution_candidate: CBT-based exposure to chewing noises with stimulus control, response-prevention, and cognitive reappraisal to reduce aversive reactivity and anger.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Graded exposure fosters inhibitory learning and habituation to triggers; cognitive reappraisal reduces automatic emotional reactivity; improved perceived controllability lowers distress.
solutions.measurement: Trigger aversiveness rating (0-10) after sessions; frequency of anger episodes; daily exposure logs and perceived controllability.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns anticipated; monitor for distress spikes; ensure gradual, voluntary exposure; escalate to clinician if aggression or impairment emerges.
solutions.mapping_query: S01 targets C01-C04 via exposure-based CBT protocols.

solutions.solution_candidate: Mindfulness-based stress reduction with daily 15-20 minute practice (body scan and mindful listening) to increase nonjudgmental awareness of triggers and reduce automatic anger responses.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced nonjudgmental awareness improves cognitive control over automatic reactions and reduces perseverative attention to sounds; supports self-compassion.
solutions.measurement: Five Facets Mindfulness Questionnaire score; momentary trigger anger rating; diary entries on attention to triggers.
solutions.safety_notes: No special safety concerns; check in with facilitator if distress increases.
solutions.mapping_query: S02 targets C02-C04; complements S01 for synergistic effects.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured sleep hygiene and circadian entrainment plan (consistent bed/wake times, light exposure management, caffeine timing) to support executive function and sensory processing.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Adequate sleep improves emotional regulation and cognitive control, reducing reactivity and intrusive cognitions associated with triggers.
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary; actigraphy; morning mood/alertness; anger reactivity logs.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; screen for sleep disorders (e.g., sleep apnea) if high risk.
solutions.mapping_query: S03 targets C02-C04 via sleep-related regulation of cognitive control.

solutions.solution_candidate: Modify sensory environment to dampen trigger exposure: soundproofing, white noise masking of chewing noises, and surface texture adjustments to reduce tactile triggers; create a trigger-minimized space.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing environmental salience of triggers lowers initial aversion and texture-related reactivity, facilitating habituation.
solutions.measurement: Subjective comfort scores during triggers; frequency/duration of trigger exposure in environment; time to calm after exposure.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure accessibility; avoid over-masking that reduces necessary exposure; guard against avoidance patterns that impede adaptation.
solutions.mapping_query: S04 targets C01 and C05 through environmental modifications.

solutions.solution_candidate: Graded auditory desensitization with controlled chewing-noise stimuli plus coping strategies (breathing, grounding) to promote habituation and reduce intrusive replay.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Systematic exposure builds tolerance and reduces perseverative auditory attention; coping skills support distress tolerance during exposure.
solutions.measurement: Discomfort threshold to chewing sounds; diaries of intrusive replay; self-rated loudness tolerance.
solutions.safety_notes: Proceed gradually; monitor distress spikes; provide clear exit strategies if triggers escalate.
solutions.mapping_query: S05 targets C01 and C03 via habituation-based auditory desensitization.

solutions.solution_candidate: Cognitive-behavioral self-compassion and non-judgmental self-talk module to reduce self-criticism and distress about sensory reactions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing self-directed distress improves distress tolerance and dampens shame-driven amplification of reactions.
solutions.measurement: Self-Compassion Scale; distress ratings; frequency of self-critical thoughts during triggers.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; ensure cultural alignment; monitor for rumination.
solutions.mapping_query: S06 targets C04 through compassion-focused cognitive strategies.

solutions.solution_candidate: Texture-focused sensory integration strategies and coping routines to reduce tactile aversion that co-occurs with auditory triggers.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved tactile tolerance reduces overall sensory overwhelm, potentially lowering cross-modal reactivity and emotional lability.
solutions.measurement: Texture aversion rating; daily tactile exposure tolerance; self-reported sensory overwhelm.
solutions.safety_notes: Progress gradually; avoid overstimulation; tailor to individual tactile preferences.
solutions.mapping_query: S07 targets C05 via tactile-sensory integration strategies.

solutions.solution_candidate: Activate social support and community resources; involve trusted individuals to provide coping conversations during triggers; use social prescribing to access supportive groups.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support reduces self-directed distress and isolation, enhancing regulation and coping during triggers.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale; frequency of supportive interactions; distress during triggers.
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain boundaries; avoid dependency; protect privacy within social networks.
solutions.mapping_query: S08 targets C04 through enhanced social connectedness.

solutions.solution_candidate: HRV biofeedback-guided breathing and autonomic regulation to dampen physiological arousal during trigger exposure, reducing anger intensity and intrusive thoughts.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved autonomic flexibility supports top-down emotional control and reduces perseverative cognitive intrusion during triggers.
solutions.measurement: HRV metrics (RMSSD); reported anger intensity; frequency of intrusive replay.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure proper guidance to avoid hyperventilation and dizziness; monitor for lightheadedness.
solutions.mapping_query: S09 targets C02-C03 via autonomic regulation techniques.

solutions.solution_candidate: Mobile app-based daily logs of trigger exposure, anger responses, and context with weekly automated feedback and adjustment prompts to tailor interventions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Self-monitoring increases awareness and enables timely coping, enabling data-driven adjustments across all targets.
solutions.measurement: Adherence rate; number of trigger events logged; changes in anger intensity; variability in responses.
solutions.safety_notes: Protect data privacy; obtain consent; provide opt-out options; avoid exposing users to triggering content unintentionally.
solutions.mapping_query: S10 targets all criteria through monitoring, feedback, and adaptive guidance.
